title: The success of efforts being made to achieve economic revival with international assistance hinges on not only economic reforms but also the implementation of political reforms.
body_lines: Proponents of constitutional economic reforms are struggling to prioritise solutions for the current socio-economic-politico imbroglio. Pohottuwa General Secretary Sagara Kariyawasam has said the economic crisis should be resolved first and then an environment created for constitutional amendments. President Gotabaya Rajapaksa banks on the Romesh de Silva Committee for the drafting of a new Constitution. The Janatha Vimukthi Peramuna, Tamil National Alliance, and Samagi Jana Balavegaya have prioritised enhanced wider constitutional reforms.

The Government is allergic to very radical changes demanded by the Gota Go Home protesters et al. Due to intense pressure, the 21st Amendment (21A) has been tabled to revive the 19th Amendment (19A). To my mind, it is a half-baked 19A Minus. It has diluted 19A, which, among other things, prevented the President from holding portfolios and limited the number of Cabinet ministers. The President has brought 42 institutions under the Ministry of Defence through the latest gazette, while admitting that he has made serious mistakes, probably disqualifying him from taking over so many responsibilities.

What the pro-democracy activists are demanding are far-reaching changes such as the President being stripped of immunity and powers to dissolve and prorogue parliament, the pardoning of convicted offenders, etc. It is well-nigh impossible for the 225 MPs to move an impeachment motion to rid of any failed President, but the latter can dissolve the parliament at the stroke of a pen!

Further, new demands are in circulation, e.g., the creation of a National Policy-Making Council, strengthening public service through depoliticisation, enhancing financial accountability (Article 148) through Committee on Public Enterprises, Committee on Public Accounts, Committee on Public Finance, etc. in the Parliament, the appointment of the Monetary Board and the Governor of the Central Bank with Constitutional Council approval, the appointment of the Ministry Secretaries, Provincial Governors, Ambassadors, et al on the advice of the PM in consultation with the Cabinet, etc. 21A does not incorporate any of these and still, government politicians and some civil society spokespersons consider 21A is the best.

One controversial demand is barring dual citizens from holding public office. A section of the Pohottuwa Group opposes this since MP Basil Rajapaksa will be affected. For instance, MP Sagara Kariyawasam, the General Secretary of Pohottuwa has said that the Constitution should not be designed to target specific individuals. He has overlooked the fact that 20A removed the bar on dual citizens for the benefit of a single individual, namely the same Basil Rajapaksa.

In a lighter vein, it is noted that many of those who are opposed to dual citizens holding public office have Prime Ministerial/Presidential dreams and consider Basil Rajapaksa as a stumbling block to them. They fish in troubled waters!

Controversial proposals of this nature had to be withdrawn to ensure a two-thirds majority when the 19A was approved by Parliament. I think something similar might happen this time around as well. Thinukural reports that Minister Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe has stated that the “provision on preventing dual citizenship holders from entering parliament is merely a proposal” may be a bargaining signal to withdraw it at the Committee Stage for a two-thirds majority to be mustered. History may repeat itself, and Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe will be the saviour.

If 21A does not receive a two-thirds majority, the President may be the happiest. Some civil society persons, without being critical of attempts to dilute the 19A, claim they accessed the country’s highest political leaders, and everyone agreed with their proposals. But concurrently we hear dissenting voices from the latter, making us wonder who is telling the truth.

Many of the proponents of constitutional reforms steadfastly believe that the economic collapse was due to political mismanagement, exacerbated by 20A, which led to the concentration of too much power in the executive presidency. They insist on reinstating the 19A even with its weaknesses. Another reason is the conviction that 21A is a halfway measure aimed at strengthening the position of the President. In the meantime, some demand a total system change and think it should be done constitutionally in one go.

Several critics believe that no system change could happen unless the President resigns. At a workshop attended also by Aragalaya representatives, this view was emphasised more than anything else. When difficulties were mentioned, they cited the removal of PM Mahinda Rajapaksa as proof of the effectiveness of pressure brought to bear on the Government and insisted the President, too, should be similarly dealt with.

The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL) wants the Executive Presidency abolished early. The public, civil society, and the Aragalaya demand that the President leave office immediately. Those canvassing for the abolition of the presidency or/and resignation question why a failed President should be allowed to be in office. Nevertheless, it is difficult under the prevailing constitutional law. This made the Aragalaya demand that the President go home. Those who are supportive of the President claim that he was elected by 6.9 million people, though the reality must be troubling them.

PM Ranil Wickremesinghe believes that the BASL is of the view that the 20A must be abolished and does not mention the President’s resignation. True. BASL proposals are extremely proactive, but they are not sacrosanct. Nor are the views of the Aragalaya or civil society views for that matter! The PM thinks that after the passage of the 21A, having restored the 19A, and strengthening the Parliament, the PM, all party leaders, and the President must decide a future course of action.

But the Minister of Justice does not want to restore 19A through 21A. Hence, how 21A strengthens the Parliament is an issue. For example, imagine a situation where the Parliament is prorogued to save roguish businesspeople or “bond rogues” or to spite another politician. It is only wishful thinking that 21A, which provides for the President to do so, will strengthen the Parliament. At a time when the President’s powers to pardon convicts have been challenged before the Supreme Court, moves to retain such power without checks and balances suggested by the BASL are absurd.

The success of efforts being made to achieve economic revival with international assistance hinges on not only economic reforms but also the implementation of political reforms. Hence the need for an approach, which supports the combination of both, as evident in the call for “a strong and conducive environment for resolving the balance of payments crisis would be to direct the country to a program of structural reforms.” They must go hand in hand, and not otherwise.

For instance, the IMF Staff Statement speaks of restoring fiscal sustainability, protecting the vulnerable and ensuring credibility of the monetary policy and exchange rate regimes; preserving financial sector stability; and states structural reforms to enhance growth and strengthen governance. Hence it can be seen that economic and political governance is on the IMF agenda.

IMF Chief Kristalina Georgieva has stated that what we undergo now is “a result of mismanagement,” and the most important thing to do is to put the island nation back on a sound macroeconomic footing. We know who bungled the macroeconomic footing and who admitted ‘mistakes’ and hence was responsible for mismanagement. The 21A tries to enable those responsible for the current mess to exercise the same powers to mismanage the economy.

The World Bank has said that it works with the IMF and other development partners in advising Sri Lanka on appropriate policies to restore economic stability until an adequate macroeconomic policy framework is in place and does not plan to offer new financing to Sri Lanka. The macroeconomic policy framework will invariably include political governance reforms too.

US Ambassador Julie Chung, at a meeting with the Speaker, has emphasised the need to carry out political reforms desired by the people and to safeguard democracy in the country. The Ambassador has said she hopes the government, including the new PM, will be able to bring about political stability and overcome the current economic crisis.

Samantha Power Administrator of USAID pledged her support to Sri Lankans and committed that USAID would help the country weather the crisis and concurrently stressed the need to urgently undertake political and economic reforms. Samantha Power’s power when she works closely with other donors such as the IMF, the World Bank, G7, and others to support Sri Lanka is assured, but her aforesaid concerns will influence her thinking.

Indian PM Narendra Modi has stated that India will continue to stand with Sri Lankans and support democracy, stability, and economic recovery in Sri Lanka. He also combined political stability with economic revival.

All foreign dignitaries have stressed the need for political stability, but SLPP General Secretary Kariyawasam is convinced otherwise. Understandably, this is to defend his political boss. He contradicts even the President who has prioritised political reforms. Against this backdrop, the onus is on PM Wickremesinghe to prove that he is in control of the situation.

The problems faced by the Government in respect of 21A are very complex. There are conflicting demands even within the Government group. Civil society does not speak with one voice. The President’s wishes are reflected in 21A, for he has confessed that it was proposed with his consent. No President will voluntarily give up powers in 20A. Discussing political power I am reminded of a quote in ‘The Power of Politicians.’

“The need to seek and retain power never goes away, and our political leaders are vulnerable to corruption just by virtue of that. … For a democratically elected politician, walking alongside every policy development, every wish for wisdom, is the thought of what its effect will be on gaining or retaining in power.”

This applies to Gotabaya Rajapaksa, Ranil Wickremesinghe, Sajith Premadasa, et al without exception. They will do everything to gain and retain power. Hence, with many manipulations, Minister Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe may be able to secure a two-thirds majority for 21A, after compromising the provisions such as those that prevent dual citizens from holding political office, unless the President and the PM defeat moves being made by Basil.

Reviewing 21A by Minister Rajapakshe and the PM before ratification could prevent mass resistance. Those in civil society and Aragalaya also should consider these practicalities of implementation without saying “To hell with the Constitution.” Aragalaya also needs to gain and retain the power to bring about changes democratically and hence the above-mentioned quote applies to it as well.

All politicians can learn a lesson from what Elaine Glaser says in Anti-Politics: “… politics is about a generality of jurisdiction, a social desire to collectively organise how things work- to have a single, agreed way of doing things.” At present we are not destined for such politics but manipulative crookedness. Let this statement be heard by all political groups.

Though I am no lawyer, to my intuition I find one roadblock to Basil Rajapaksa and company. It is in Article 78(3) which was introduced by the Gotabaya Rajapaksa government when 20A was introduced. It says as follows: “(3) Any amendment proposed to a Bill in Parliament shall not deviate from the merits and principles of that Bill.”

It will be the inviolable, sacrosanct right for the Opposition to harp on it if Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe or a Basil Rajapaksa supporter attempts to withdraw the “proposal” (as the former has stated in Thinukural) to be constitutionally crooked to obtain two-thirds at approval. It will be decided by the House or a Court of Law one day when the “merits” and “principles” of the Bill are decided. In the House, I do not expect merit and principles to reign. Anyway, Parliamentarians may make use of this provision to prevent anyone from bringing an amendment at the Committee Stage enabling dual citizens to hold political office. Let us keep our fingers crossed.

Civil society also should mind the criticism against them as carry-overs of what Hirunika Premachandra started at Mirihana. It must be aware that it should not allow the politicians to take it for a ride with a promise to reduce presidential powers later, to secure a two-thirds majority for the 21A. It will not happen as many in politics whether in the Government or the Opposition aspire to use these draconian powers and will fall back at the crucial juncture because as quoted above, they need to ‘gain’ and ‘retain’ power.

To PM Wickremesinghe one may say, “Sir, this is the last opportunity for you to make good governance a reality, and do not allow it to be whisked away by manipulations. If you do not achieve it now, you will be called a failure who sinned against democratic good governance values, for which you are beholden even in the international arena.”

si.title: ජාත්‍යන්තර සහය ඇතිව ආර්ථික පුනර්ජීවනයක් ඇති කර ගැනීමට දරන උත්සාහයේ සාර්ථකත්වය රඳා පවතින්නේ ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ පමණක් නොව දේශපාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම ද මත ය.
body_lines: ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාමය ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී සිටින්නන් වත්මන් සමාජ-ආර්ථික-දේශපාලන අවුල් සහගත තත්ත්වය සඳහා විසඳුම් සඳහා ප්‍රමුඛත්වය දීමට අරගල කරමින් සිටිති. ප්‍රථමයෙන් ආර්ථික අර්බුදය විසඳා පසුව ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධන සඳහා පරිසරයක් නිර්මාණය කළ යුතු බව පොහොට්ටුව ප්‍රධාන ලේකම් සාගර කාරියවසම් මහතා පවසයි. නව ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවක් සම්පාදනය කිරීම සඳහා ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ මහතා රොමේෂ් ද සිල්වා කමිටුවට සම්බන්ධ වේ. ජනතා විමුක්ති පෙරමුණ, දෙමළ ජාතික සන්ධානය සහ සමගි ජන බලවේගය වැඩි දියුණු කළ පුළුල් ව්‍යවස්ථා ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ සඳහා ප්‍රමුඛත්වය දී ඇත.

ගෝටා ගෝ හෝම් විරෝධතාකරුවන් සහ වෙනත් අය විසින් ඉල්ලා සිටින ඉතා රැඩිකල් වෙනස්කම් රජයට අසාත්මික ය. දැඩි පීඩනය හේතුවෙන් 19 වැනි සංශෝධනයට (19A) පණ දීමට 21 වැනි සංශෝධනය (21A) ඉදිරිපත් කර ඇත. මගේ අදහස නම්, එය බාගෙට පුළුස්සන ලද 19A අඩුවීමක්. එය 19A දියාරු කර ඇති අතර එමඟින් ජනාධිපතිවරයාට විෂය පථයන් දැරීමට නොහැකි වූ අතර කැබිනට් අමාත්‍යවරුන් සංඛ්‍යාව සීමා කළේය. ජනාධිපතිවරයා විසින් නවතම ගැසට් පත්‍රය මගින් ආයතන 42ක් ආරක්‍ෂක අමාත්‍යාංශය යටතට ගෙන ඇත්තේ තමන් බරපතළ වැරදි සිදු කර ඇති බව පිළිගන්නා අතරම මෙතරම් වගකීම් දැරීමට නුසුදුස්සෙකු විය හැකිය.

ප‍්‍රජාතන්ත‍්‍රවාදී ක‍්‍රියාකාරීන් ඉල්ලා සිටින්නේ ජනාධිපතිවරයාට පාර්ලිමේන්තුව විසුරුවා හැරීමේ සහ කල් දැමීමේ බලතල ඉවත් කිරීම, වැරදිකරුවන්ට සමාව දීම යනාදී දුරදිග යන වෙනස්කම් ය. පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්තී‍්‍රවරුන් 225 දෙනාට එය ගෙනයාමට නොහැකි තරම් ය. දෝෂාභියෝගය ඕනෑම අසාර්ථක ජනාධිපතිවරයෙකු ඉවත් කිරීමට, නමුත් දෙවැන්නාට පෑනෙන් පාර්ලිමේන්තුව විසුරුවා හැරිය හැකිය!

තවද, නව ඉල්ලීම් සංසරණය වෙමින් පවතී, උදා: ජාතික ප්‍රතිපත්ති සම්පාදන කවුන්සිලයක් ඇති කිරීම, දේශපාලනීකරණයෙන් රාජ්‍ය සේවය ශක්තිමත් කිරීම, රාජ්‍ය ව්‍යවසාය පිළිබඳ කාරක සභාව, රාජ්‍ය ගිණුම් පිළිබඳ කාරක සභාව, රාජ්‍ය මුදල් පිළිබඳ කාරක සභාව හරහා මූල්‍ය වගවීම ඉහළ නැංවීම (148 වැනි වගන්තිය). පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේදී, ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සභාවේ අනුමැතිය ඇතිව මුදල් මණ්ඩලය සහ මහ බැංකු අධිපතිවරයා පත් කිරීම, අමාත්‍යාංශ ලේකම්වරුන්, පළාත් ආණ්ඩුකාරවරුන්, තානාපතිවරුන් සහ වෙනත් අය කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලය සමඟ සාකච්ඡා කර අගමැතිවරයාගේ උපදෙස් මත පත් කිරීම යනාදිය. 21A හි මේ කිසිවක් අන්තර්ගත නොවන අතර තවමත් රජයේ දේශපාලනඥයන් සහ ඇතැම් සිවිල් සමාජ ප්‍රකාශකයින් 21A හොඳම දේ ලෙස සලකයි.

එක් මතභේදාත්මක ඉල්ලීමක් නම් ද්විත්ව පුරවැසියන්ට රාජ්‍ය නිලතල දැරීමට නොහැකි වීමයි. පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂ මහතාට බලපෑම් එල්ල වන නිසා පොහොට්ටුවේ කණ්ඩායමක් මෙයට විරුද්ධයි. උදාහරණයක් වශයෙන්, පොහොට්ටුවේ ප්‍රධාන ලේකම් සාගර කාරියවසම් මන්ත්‍රීවරයා පවසා ඇත්තේ නිශ්චිත පුද්ගලයන් ඉලක්ක කර ගනිමින් ව්‍යවස්ථාව සකස් නොකළ යුතු බවයි. 20A විසින් ද්විත්ව පුරවැසියන් සඳහා වූ තහනම ඉවත් කර ඇත්තේ තනි පුද්ගලයකුගේ එනම් එම බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂගේම ප්‍රයෝජනය සඳහා බව ඔහු නොසලකා හැර තිබේ.

ද්විත්ව පුරවැසියන් රාජ්‍ය නිලතල දැරීමට විරුද්ධ බොහෝ දෙනෙකුට අගමැති/ජනාධිපති සිහින ඇති බවත් බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂ ඔවුන්ට බාධාවක් ලෙස සලකන බවත් සැහැල්ලු ශිරා තුළ සටහන් වේ. ඔවුන් මසුන් ඇල්ලීම කැළඹිලි සහිත ජලයේ!

19A ට පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේ අනුමැතිය ලැබුණු විට තුනෙන් දෙකක බලය තහවුරු කර ගැනීම සඳහා මෙවැනි ආන්දෝලනාත්මක යෝජනා ඉල්ලා අස්කර ගැනීමට සිදු විය. මම හිතන්නේ මේ පාරත් ඒ වගේ දෙයක් වෙන්න පුළුවන්. “ද්විත්ව පුරවැසිභාවය දරන්නන් පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට පැමිණීම වැලැක්වීමේ විධිවිධානය යෝජනාවක් පමණක්” බව අමාත්‍ය විජයදාස රාජපක්ෂ ප්‍රකාශ කර ඇති බව තිනුකුරල් වාර්තා කරයි. ඉතිහාසය නැවත සිදු විය හැකි අතර, විජයදාස රාජපක්ෂ ගැලවුම්කාරයා වනු ඇත.

21A ට තුනෙන් දෙකක බලයක් නොලැබුණොත් වඩාත්ම සතුටු වන්නේ ජනාධිපතිවරයා විය හැකියි. සමහර සිවිල් සමාජ පුද්ගලයන්, 19A දියාරු කිරීමේ උත්සාහයන් විවේචනය නොකර, තමන් රටේ ඉහළම දේශපාලන නායකයින් වෙත ප්‍රවේශ වූ බව පවසන අතර, ඔවුන්ගේ යෝජනාවලට සියලු දෙනා එකඟ වූහ. නමුත් ඊට සමගාමීව දෙවැන්නාගෙන් විසම්මුතික හඬවල් අපට ඇසෙන අතර, ඇත්ත කියන්නේ කවුරුන්ද යන්න අපව පුදුමයට පත් කරවයි.

ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී සිටින බොහෝ දෙනා ස්ථීර ලෙස විශ්වාස කරන්නේ, විධායක ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය තුළ ඕනෑවට වඩා බලය සංකේන්ද්‍රණය වීමට තුඩු දුන් 20A මගින් උග්‍ර වූ දේශපාලන අවපාලනය හේතුවෙන් ආර්ථික කඩාවැටීම සිදුවූ බවයි. 19A එහි දුර්වලතා සමඟ පවා නැවත ස්ථාපිත කළ යුතු බව ඔවුහු අවධාරණය කරති. තවත් හේතුවක් වන්නේ 21A යනු ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ ස්ථාවරය ශක්තිමත් කිරීම අරමුණු කරගත් අතරමග පියවරක් බවට ඒත්තු ගැන්වීමයි. මේ අතර ඇතැමුන් සමස්ත ක්‍රමයේ වෙනසක් ඉල්ලා සිටින අතර එය ව්‍යවස්ථානුකූලව එකවර කළ යුතු යැයි සිතති.

විවේචකයින් කිහිප දෙනෙකු විශ්වාස කරන්නේ ජනාධිපතිවරයා ඉල්ලා අස් නොවන්නේ නම් ක්‍රමයේ වෙනසක් සිදු නොවන බවයි. අරගලය නියෝජිතයන් ද සහභාගි වූ වැඩමුළුවකදී අන් සියල්ලටම වඩා අවධාරණය කළේ මෙම මතයයි. දුෂ්කරතා ගැන සඳහන් කළ විට, ඔවුන් අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ ඉවත් කිරීම රජයට ගෙන එන බලපෑමේ ඵලදායිතාව පිළිබඳ සාක්ෂියක් ලෙස සඳහන් කළ අතර ජනාධිපතිවරයාට ද ඒ හා සමානව කටයුතු කළ යුතු බව ඔවුහු අවධාරණය කළහ.

ශ්‍රී ලංකා නීතීඥ සංගමයට (BASL) අවශ්‍ය වන්නේ විධායක ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය ඉක්මනින්ම අහෝසි කරන ලෙසයි. මහජනතාව, සිවිල් සමාජය සහ අරගලය ඉල්ලා සිටින්නේ ජනාධිපතිවරයා වහාම ධුරයෙන් ඉවත් විය යුතු බවයි. ජනාධිපති ධූරය අහෝසි කිරීම හෝ/සහ ඉල්ලා අස්වීම ඉල්ලා උද්ඝෝෂණ කරන අය ප්‍රශ්න කරන්නේ අසාර්ථක ජනාධිපතිවරයෙකුට ධූරයේ සිටීමට ඉඩ දිය යුත්තේ මන්දැයි යන්නයි. එසේ වුවද පවතින ව්‍යවස්ථාපිත නීතිය යටතේ එය දුෂ්කර ය. මේ නිසා අරගලයා ජනාධිපතිවරයාට ගෙදර යන්නැයි ඉල්ලා සිටියේය. ජනාධිපතිවරයාට හිතවත් අය කියා සිටින්නේ ඔහු ලක්‍ෂ 69 ක ජනතාවකින් තේරී පත් වූ බවයි, නමුත් යථාර්ථය ඔවුන්ට කරදරයක් විය යුතුය.

20A අහෝසි කළ යුතු බව ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණේ අදහස බවත් ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ ඉල්ලා අස්වීම ගැන සඳහන් නොකරන බවත් අගමැති රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ විශ්වාස කරයි. සැබෑ. BASL යෝජනා අතිශයින්ම ක්‍රියාකාරී වන නමුත් ඒවා පරිශුද්ධ නොවේ. ඒ සඳහා අරගලයාගේ අදහස් හෝ සිවිල් සමාජ මතවාද නොවේ! අග්‍රාමාත්‍යවරයා සිතන්නේ 21A සම්මත කිරීමෙන් පසුව, 19A ප්‍රතිෂ්ඨාපනය කර, පාර්ලිමේන්තුව ශක්තිමත් කිරීමෙන් පසුව, අග්‍රාමාත්‍යවරයා, සියලු පක්ෂ නායකයින් සහ ජනාධිපතිවරයා ඉදිරි ක්‍රියාමාර්ග තීරණය කළ යුතු බවයි.

නමුත් අධිකරණ ඇමතිවරයාට අවශ්‍ය නැහැ 21ඒ හරහා 19ඒ ප්‍රතිෂ්ඨාපනය කරන්න. ඒ නිසා 21A පාර්ලිමේන්තුව ශක්තිමත් කරන්නේ කොහොමද කියන එක ප්‍රශ්නයක්. නිදසුනක් වශයෙන්, තක්කඩි ව්‍යාපාරිකයන් හෝ “බැඳුම්කර තක්කඩියන්” බේරා ගැනීමට හෝ වෙනත් දේශපාලකයෙකුට එරෙහිව ක්‍රියා කිරීමට පාර්ලිමේන්තුව කල් තබන තත්වයක් ගැන සිතන්න. ජනාධිපතිවරයාට එසේ කිරීමට විධිවිධාන සලසන 21A මගින් පාර්ලිමේන්තුව ශක්තිමත් කරනු ඇතැයි සිතීම ප්‍රාර්ථනාවක් පමණි. වරදකරුවන්ට සමාව දීමට ජනාධිපතිවරයාට ඇති බලතල ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය හමුවේ අභියෝගයට ලක්ව ඇති අවස්ථාවක, BASL විසින් යෝජනා කරන ලද චෙක්පත් සහ තුලනයකින් තොරව එම බලය රඳවා ගැනීමට ගන්නා පියවර විකාර සහගතය.

ජාත්‍යන්තර සහය ඇතිව ආර්ථික පුනර්ජීවනයක් ඇති කර ගැනීමට දරන උත්සාහයේ සාර්ථකත්වය රඳා පවතින්නේ ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ පමණක් නොව දේශපාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීම ද මත ය. එබැවින්, "ගෙවීම් ශේෂ අර්බුදය විසඳීම සඳහා ශක්තිමත් සහ හිතකර පරිසරයක් සඳහා වන කැඳවීම තුලින් පැහැදිලි වන පරිදි, මේ දෙකේම එකතුවට සහාය වන ප්‍රවේශයක අවශ්‍යතාවය වන්නේ රට ව්‍යුහාත්මක ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ වැඩසටහනකට යොමු කිරීමයි." ඔවුන් අත්වැල් බැඳගෙන යා යුතු අතර, වෙනත් ආකාරයකින් නොවේ.

නිදසුනක් වශයෙන්, IMF කාර්ය මණ්ඩල ප්‍රකාශය මූල්‍ය තිරසාරභාවය ප්‍රතිෂ්ඨාපනය කිරීම, අවදානමට ලක්විය හැකි අය ආරක්ෂා කිරීම සහ මුදල් ප්‍රතිපත්ති සහ විනිමය අනුපාත පාලන ක්‍රමවල විශ්වසනීයත්වය සහතික කිරීම ගැන කතා කරයි; මූල්ය අංශයේ ස්ථාවරත්වය ආරක්ෂා කිරීම; සහ වර්ධනය වැඩි දියුණු කිරීමට සහ පාලනය ශක්තිමත් කිරීමට ව්‍යුහාත්මක ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ ප්‍රකාශ කරයි. එබැවින් ආර්ථික හා දේශපාලන පාලනය ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදලේ න්‍යාය පත්‍රයේ ඇති බව දැකිය හැකිය.

ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදලේ ප්‍රධානී ක්‍රිස්ටලිනා ජෝර්ජිවා ප්‍රකාශ කර ඇත්තේ අප දැන් අත්විඳින්නේ "වැරදි කළමනාකරණයේ ප්‍රතිඵලයක්" බවත්, කළ යුතු වැදගත්ම දෙය වන්නේ දිවයින ජාතිය නැවත හොඳ සාර්ව ආර්ථික පදනමක් මත තැබීමයි. සාර්ව ආර්ථික පදනම අවුල් කළේ කවුරුන්ද සහ 'වැරදි' පිළිගත්තේ කවුරුන්ද, එබැවින් වැරදි කළමනාකරණයට වගකිව යුත්තේ කවුරුන්ද යන්න අපි දනිමු. 21A උත්සාහ කරන්නේ වත්මන් අවුලට වගකිව යුත්තන්ට ආර්ථිකය වැරදි ලෙස කළමනාකරණය කිරීම සඳහා එම බලතල භාවිතා කිරීමට ඉඩ සැලසීමට ය.

ප්‍රමාණවත් සාර්ව ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිපත්ති රාමුවක් ක්‍රියාත්මක වන තෙක් ආර්ථික ස්ථාවරත්වය යථා තත්ත්වයට පත් කිරීම සඳහා සුදුසු ප්‍රතිපත්ති පිළිබඳව ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට උපදෙස් දීම සඳහා IMF සහ අනෙකුත් සංවර්ධන හවුල්කරුවන් සමඟ කටයුතු කරන බවත් ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට නව මූල්‍ය පහසුකම් ලබා දීමට සැලසුම් නොකරන බවත් ලෝක බැංකුව පවසා තිබේ. සාර්ව ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිපත්ති රාමුවට දේශපාලන පාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ ද ඇතුළත් වේ.

ජනතාව අපේක්ෂා කරන දේශපාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ සිදු කර රටේ ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදය සුරැකීමේ අවශ්‍යතාව අමෙරිකානු තානාපතිනි ජුලී චුන්ග් මහත්මිය කථානායකවරයා සමඟ පැවැති සාකච්ඡාවකදී අවධාරණය කර තිබේ. නව අගමැතිවරයා ඇතුළු රජයට දේශපාලන ස්ථාවරත්වයක් ඇති කිරීමටත් වත්මන් ආර්ථික අර්බුදයෙන් ගොඩ ඒමට හැකිවනු ඇතැයි තමන් බලාපොරොත්තු වන බව තානාපතිනිය පවසා තිබේ.

USAID හි සමන්තා පවර් පරිපාලක ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයින්ට සිය සහය ලබා දෙන බවට ප්‍රතිඥා දුන් අතර USAID අර්බුදයෙන් මිදීමට රටට උපකාර කරන බවට කැප වූ අතර දේශපාලන හා ආර්ථික ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ කඩිනමින් සිදු කිරීමේ අවශ්‍යතාවයද අවධාරණය කළේය. සමන්තා පවර් ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට සහාය වීම සඳහා IMF, ලෝක බැංකුව, G7 වැනි අනෙකුත් පරිත්‍යාගශීලීන් සමඟ සමීපව කටයුතු කරන විට ඇයගේ බලය සහතිකයි, නමුත් ඇයගේ පෙර කී සැලකිල්ල ඇගේ චින්තනයට බලපානු ඇත.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදය, ස්ථාවරත්වය සහ ආර්ථික ප්‍රකෘතිය සඳහා ඉන්දියාව අඛණ්ඩව ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන් සමඟ සිට සහයෝගය දක්වන බව ඉන්දීය අග්‍රාමාත්‍ය නරේන්ද්‍ර මෝදි ප්‍රකාශ කළා. ඔහු දේශපාලන ස්ථාවරත්වය ආර්ථික පුනර්ජීවනය සමඟ ඒකාබද්ධ කළේය.

සියලුම විදේශීය ප්‍රභූවරුන් දේශපාලන ස්ථාවරත්වයේ අවශ්‍යතාවය අවධාරණය කර ඇතත්, ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණේ ප්‍රධාන ලේකම් කාරියවසම් වෙනත් ආකාරයකින් ඒත්තු ගැන්වී ඇත. මෙය ඔහුගේ දේශපාලන ලොක්කා ආරක්ෂා කිරීමට බව තේරුම් ගත හැකිය. ඔහු දේශපාලන ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණවලට මුල්තැන දුන් ජනාධිපතිවරයාට පවා පටහැනිය. මේ පසුබිම තුළ තත්ත්වය පාලනය කරන්නේ තමන් බව ඔප්පු කිරීමේ වගකීම අගමැති වික්‍රමසිංහට පැවරී ඇත.

21A සම්බන්ධයෙන් රජය මුහුණ දෙන ගැටලු ඉතා සංකීර්ණයි. රජයේ කණ්ඩායම තුළ පවා පරස්පර ඉල්ලීම් තිබේ. සිවිල් සමාජය එක හඬින් කතා කරන්නේ නැහැ. ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ කැමැත්ත 21A හි පිළිබිඹු වේ, මන්ද එය ඔහුගේ අනුමැතිය ඇතිව යෝජනා කළ බව ඔහු පාපොච්චාරණය කර ඇත. කිසිම ජනාධිපතිවරයෙක් 20A හි බලතල ස්වේච්ඡාවෙන් අත්හරින්නේ නැහැ. දේශපාලන බලය ගැන කතා කරන විට මට මතක් වන්නේ ‘දේශපාලකයන්ගේ බලය’ කෘතියේ ඇති කියමනකි.

“බලය සෙවීමේ හා රඳවා ගැනීමේ අවශ්‍යතාවය කිසි විටෙකත් පහව යන්නේ නැත, අපගේ දේශපාලන නායකයින් දූෂණයට ගොදුරු වන්නේ එය නිසා පමණි. ... ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදීව තේරී පත් වූ දේශපාලකයෙකුට, සෑම ප්‍රතිපත්තිමය වර්ධනයක්, ප්‍රඥාව සඳහා වූ සෑම ප්‍රාර්ථනාවක්ම සමඟින් ගමන් කිරීම, බලය ලබා ගැනීමට හෝ රඳවා ගැනීමට එහි බලපෑම කුමක් ද යන්නයි.

මෙය ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ, රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ, සජිත් ප්‍රේමදාස ආදීන්ට ව්‍යතිරේකයකින් තොරව අදාළ වේ. බලය ලබා ගැනීමට සහ බලය රඳවා ගැනීමට ඔවුන් සෑම දෙයක්ම කරනු ඇත. එහෙයින්, බොහෝ උපාමාරු සමඟින්, ද්විත්ව පුරවැසියන්ට දේශපාලන ධූර දැරීම වළක්වන විධිවිධාන යටපත් කර, ජනාධිපති සහ අගමැති බැසිල්ගේ ක්‍රියාදාමයන් පරාජය නොකළහොත් 21A සඳහා තුනෙන් දෙකක බලයක් ලබා ගැනීමට අමාත්‍ය විජයදාස රාජපක්ෂට හැකිවනු ඇත. .

අනුමත කිරීමට පෙර 21A අමාත්‍ය රාජපක්ෂ සහ අගමැති විසින් සමාලෝචනය කිරීමෙන් මහජන විරෝධය වැළැක්විය හැකිය. සිවිල් සමාජයේ සහ අරගලයේ සිටින අය ද "ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාවෙන් මගුලට" නොකියා ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමේ මෙම ප්‍රායෝගික කරුණු සලකා බැලිය යුතුය. ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදීව වෙනස්කම් ඇතිකිරීමේ බලය අරගලයටද ලබාගත යුතු අතර එම නිසා ඉහත කී උද්ධෘතය ඊටද අදාළ වේ.

Anti Politics හි Elaine Glaser පවසන දෙයින් සියලුම දේශපාලකයන්ට පාඩමක් උගත හැක: "... දේශපාලනය යනු සාමාන්‍ය අධිකරණ බලයක්, දේවල් ක්‍රියාත්මක වන ආකාරය සාමූහිකව සංවිධානය කිරීමේ සමාජ ආශාවක් - තනි, එකඟ වූ දේවල් කිරීමට. වර්තමානයේ අපට දෛවෝපගත වී ඇත්තේ එවැනි දේශපාලනයක් නොව උපාමාරු තක්කඩිකමකි. මෙම ප්‍රකාශය සියලුම දේශපාලන කණ්ඩායම්වලට අසන්නට ලැබේවා.

මම නීතීඥයෙක් නොවුණත් මගේ බුද්ධියට අනුව මට බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂ ඇතුළු පිරිසට එක් මාර්ග බාධකයක් හමුවෙනවා. ඒ 20A ගෙන එන විට ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ ආණ්ඩුව ගෙන ආ 78(3) වගන්තියේය. එය මෙසේ කියයි: "(3) පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේ පනත් කෙටුම්පතක් සඳහා යෝජිත ඕනෑම සංශෝධනයක් එම පනත් කෙටුම්පතේ කුසලතා සහ මූලධර්මවලින් බැහැර නොවිය යුතුය."

තුනෙන් දෙකේ අනුමැතිය ලබා ගැනීම සඳහා ව්‍යවස්ථානුකූලව තක්කඩි කළ “යෝජනාව” (කලින් තිනුකුරලයේ සඳහන් කර ඇති පරිදි) ඉල්ලා අස්කර ගැනීමට විජයදාස රාජපක්ෂ හෝ බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂ හිතවාදියෙකු උත්සාහ කරන්නේ නම් එය විරුද්ධ පක්ෂයට වීණා කිරීම උල්ලංඝනය කළ නොහැකි පරිශුද්ධ අයිතියක් වනු ඇත. . පනත් කෙටුම්පතේ "කුසලතා" සහ "මූලධර්ම" තීරණය වූ දිනක එය සභාව හෝ අධිකරණය විසින් තීරණය කරනු ලැබේ. සභාව තුළ මම කුසල් සහ ප්‍රතිපත්ති රජ කිරීමට බලාපොරොත්තු නොවෙමි. කෙසේ වෙතත්, ද්විත්ව පුරවැසියන්ට දේශපාලන නිලතල දැරීමට හැකිවන පරිදි කාරක සභා අවස්ථාවේදී කිසිවකුට සංශෝධනයක් ගෙන ඒම වැළැක්වීම සඳහා පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රීවරුන්ට මෙම විධිවිධානය භාවිතා කළ හැකිය. අපි ඇඟිලි තුඩු තබා ගනිමු.

හිරුණිකා ප්‍රේමචන්ද්‍ර මිරිහානේදී ආරම්භ කළ දේ රැගෙන යන්නන් ලෙස ඔවුන්ට එරෙහිව එල්ල වන විවේචන සිවිල් සමාජයද සැලකිල්ලට ගත යුතුය. 21A ට තුනෙන් දෙකක බලයක් ලබා ගැනීම සඳහා ජනාධිපති බලතල පසුව අඩු කරන බවට පොරොන්දු වී දේශපාලකයන්ට එය සවාරියක් ගැනීමට ඉඩ නොතැබිය යුතු බව එය දැන සිටිය යුතුය. ආණ්ඩුවේ හෝ විපක්ෂයේ හෝ වේවා දේශපාලනයේ සිටින බොහෝ දෙනෙක් මෙම කුරිරු බලතල භාවිතා කිරීමට අපේක්ෂා කරන අතර තීරණාත්මක සන්ධිස්ථානයට ආපසු වැටෙනු ඇත, මන්ද ඉහත සඳහන් කළ පරිදි ඔවුන්ට බලය 'ලබා ගැනීමට' සහ 'තබා ගැනීමට' අවශ්‍ය බැවිනි.

අගමැති වික්‍රමසිංහට කෙනෙක් මෙසේ පැවසිය හැකිය, “සර්, යහපාලනය යථාර්ථයක් බවට පත් කිරීමට ඔබට ඇති අවසාන අවස්ථාව මෙයයි, එය උපාමාරුවලින් ගසා කෑමට ඉඩ නොදෙන්න. ඔබ දැන් එය සාක්ෂාත් කර නොගන්නේ නම්, ඔබ ජාත්‍යන්තර තලයේ පවා අවධානයට ලක්වන ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී යහපාලන සාරධර්මවලට එරෙහිව පව් කළ අසාර්ථකයෙකු ලෙස හඳුන්වනු ඇත.

ta.title: சர்வதேச உதவியுடன் பொருளாதார மறுமலர்ச்சியை அடைவதற்கான முயற்சிகளின் வெற்றியானது பொருளாதார சீர்திருத்தங்களை மட்டுமல்ல, அரசியல் சீர்திருத்தங்களை செயல்படுத்துவதையும் சார்ந்துள்ளது.
body_lines: அரசியலமைப்பு பொருளாதார சீர்திருத்தங்களை ஆதரிப்பவர்கள் தற்போதைய சமூக-பொருளாதார-அரசியல் குழப்பத்திற்கான தீர்வுகளுக்கு முன்னுரிமை அளிக்க போராடுகின்றனர். பொருளாதார நெருக்கடிக்கு முதலில் தீர்வு காணப்பட்டு பின்னர் அரசியலமைப்பு திருத்தங்களை மேற்கொள்ளும் சூழலை உருவாக்க வேண்டும் என பொஹொட்டுவ பொதுச் செயலாளர் சாகர காரியவசம் தெரிவித்துள்ளார். புதிய அரசியலமைப்பை உருவாக்குவதற்கான ரொமேஷ் டி சில்வா குழுவில் ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச வங்கிகளை சேர்த்துள்ளார். ஜனதா விமுக்தி பெரமுனா, தமிழ்த் தேசியக் கூட்டமைப்பு மற்றும் சமகி ஜன பலவேகய ஆகியன மேம்படுத்தப்பட்ட பரந்த அரசியலமைப்பு சீர்திருத்தங்களுக்கு முன்னுரிமை அளித்துள்ளன.

கோட்டா கோ ஹோம் எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் மற்றும் பலர் கோரும் தீவிரமான மாற்றங்களால் அரசுக்கு ஒவ்வாமை உள்ளது. கடுமையான அழுத்தத்தின் காரணமாக, 19வது திருத்தத்திற்கு (19A) புத்துயிர் அளிக்கும் வகையில் 21வது திருத்தம் (21A) சமர்ப்பிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. என் மனதில், அது அரை வேகவைத்த 19A மைனஸ். இது 19A ஐ நீர்த்துப்போகச் செய்துள்ளது, இது மற்றவற்றுடன், ஜனாதிபதியை இலாகாக்களை வைத்திருப்பதைத் தடுத்தது மற்றும் அமைச்சரவை அமைச்சர்களின் எண்ணிக்கையை மட்டுப்படுத்தியது. ஜனாதிபதி 42 நிறுவனங்களை பாதுகாப்பு அமைச்சின் கீழ் கொண்டுவந்துள்ளார். அதேவேளையில், தான் பாரிய தவறுகளை இழைத்துள்ளதாக ஒப்புக்கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி, பல பொறுப்புகளை ஏற்கும் தகுதியை இழந்துள்ளார்.

ஜனநாயக சார்பு ஆர்வலர்கள் கோருவது என்னவென்றால், ஜனாதிபதியின் விதிவிலக்கு மற்றும் பாராளுமன்றத்தை கலைக்கும் மற்றும் ஒத்திவைக்கும் அதிகாரங்கள் பறிக்கப்படுவது, தண்டனை பெற்ற குற்றவாளிகளுக்கு மன்னிப்பு வழங்குவது போன்ற தொலைநோக்கு மாற்றங்களைச் செய்ய வேண்டும். 225 எம்.பி.க்களால் நகர்த்துவது கிட்டத்தட்ட சாத்தியமற்றது. எந்த ஒரு தோல்வியுற்ற ஜனாதிபதியையும் பதவி நீக்கம் செய்வதற்கான தீர்மானம், ஆனால் பிந்தையவர் பாராளுமன்றத்தை பேனாவின் அடியில் கலைக்க முடியும்!

மேலும், புதிய கோரிக்கைகள் புழக்கத்தில் உள்ளன, எ.கா., தேசிய கொள்கை உருவாக்கும் குழுவை உருவாக்குதல், அரசியலற்றமயமாக்கல் மூலம் பொது சேவையை வலுப்படுத்துதல், பொது நிறுவனங்களுக்கான குழு, பொதுக் கணக்குகள் குழு, பொது நிதிக் குழு போன்றவற்றின் மூலம் நிதிப் பொறுப்புணர்வை மேம்படுத்துதல் (பிரிவு 148). பாராளுமன்றத்தில், அரசியலமைப்பு சபையின் அங்கீகாரத்துடன் நாணயச் சபை மற்றும் மத்திய வங்கியின் ஆளுநரை நியமித்தல், அமைச்சின் செயலாளர்கள், மாகாண ஆளுநர்கள், தூதுவர்கள் மற்றும் பலர் நியமனம், அமைச்சரவையுடன் கலந்தாலோசித்து பிரதமரின் ஆலோசனையின் பேரில், முதலியன 21A, இவற்றில் எதையும் இணைக்கவில்லை, இன்னும், அரசாங்க அரசியல்வாதிகள் மற்றும் சில சிவில் சமூகப் பேச்சாளர்கள் 21A சிறந்ததாகக் கருதுகின்றனர்.

ஒரு சர்ச்சைக்குரிய கோரிக்கை இரட்டை குடியுரிமை பெற்றவர்கள் பொதுப் பதவியில் இருப்பதைத் தடுக்கிறது. பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் பசில் ராஜபக்ஷ பாதிக்கப்படுவார் என்பதால் பொஹொட்டுவ குழுவின் ஒரு பிரிவினர் இதனை எதிர்க்கின்றனர். உதாரணமாக, குறிப்பிட்ட நபர்களை இலக்கு வைத்து அரசியலமைப்பு உருவாக்கப்படக் கூடாது என பொஹொட்டுவவின் பொதுச் செயலாளர் பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் சாகர காரியவசம் தெரிவித்துள்ளார். 20A இரட்டைக் குடியுரிமைக்கான தடையை நீக்கியது, அதே பசில் ராஜபக்சவின் நலனுக்காக என்ற உண்மையை அவர் கவனிக்கவில்லை.

இரட்டைக் குடியுரிமை பெற்றவர்கள் அரசுப் பதவிகளை வகிப்பதை எதிர்க்கும் பலர், பிரதமர்/ஜனாதிபதி கனவுகளைக் கொண்டிருப்பதாகவும், பசில் ராஜபக்ஷவை தங்களுக்கு முட்டுக்கட்டையாகக் கருதுவதாகவும் இலகுவான முறையில் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளது. கலங்கிய நீரில் மீன் பிடிக்கிறார்கள்!

19A பாராளுமன்றத்தால் அங்கீகரிக்கப்பட்ட போது மூன்றில் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மையை உறுதிப்படுத்த இந்த வகையான சர்ச்சைக்குரிய முன்மொழிவுகளை திரும்பப் பெற வேண்டியிருந்தது. இந்த முறையும் அப்படி ஏதாவது நடக்கலாம் என்று நினைக்கிறேன். "இரட்டைக் குடியுரிமை பெற்றவர்கள் பாராளுமன்றத்திற்குள் பிரவேசிப்பதைத் தடுப்பது ஒரு பிரேரணை மட்டுமே" என அமைச்சர் விஜயதாச ராஜபக்ஷ தெரிவித்ததாக தினக்குரல் தெரிவிக்கின்றது. மூன்றில் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மையை பெறுவதற்காக குழுநிலையில் அதனை வாபஸ் பெறுவதற்கான பேரம் பேசும் சமிக்ஞையாக இருக்கலாம். வரலாறு மீண்டும் நிகழலாம், விஜயதாச ராஜபக்ஷ மீட்பராக இருப்பார்.

21A மூன்றில் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மையைப் பெறவில்லை என்றால், ஜனாதிபதி மிகவும் மகிழ்ச்சியாக இருக்கலாம். சில சிவில் சமூக நபர்கள், 19A ஐ நீர்த்துப்போகச் செய்யும் முயற்சிகளை விமர்சிக்காமல், நாட்டின் மிக உயர்ந்த அரசியல் தலைவர்களை அணுகியதாகக் கூறுகின்றனர், மேலும் அனைவரும் அவர்களின் முன்மொழிவுகளை ஏற்றுக்கொண்டனர். ஆனால் அதே சமயம் பிந்தையவர்களிடமிருந்து மாறுபட்ட குரல்களைக் கேட்கிறோம், யார் உண்மையைச் சொல்கிறார்கள் என்று நம்மை ஆச்சரியப்படுத்துகிறது.

அரசியலமைப்பு சீர்திருத்தங்களை ஆதரிப்பவர்களில் பலர், பொருளாதார சரிவு அரசியல் தவறான நிர்வாகத்தால் ஏற்பட்டது என்று உறுதியாக நம்புகிறார்கள், இது 20A ஆல் மோசமாக்கப்பட்டது, இது நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறையில் அதிக அதிகாரம் குவிவதற்கு வழிவகுத்தது. 19A ஐ அதன் பலவீனங்களுடனும் மீண்டும் நிலைநிறுத்த வேண்டும் என்று அவர்கள் வலியுறுத்துகின்றனர். மற்றொரு காரணம், 21A என்பது ஜனாதிபதியின் பதவியை வலுப்படுத்துவதை நோக்கமாகக் கொண்ட ஒரு பாதி நடவடிக்கையாகும். இதற்கிடையில், சிலர் ஒட்டுமொத்த அமைப்பு மாற்றத்தை கோருகின்றனர் மற்றும் அது ஒரே நேரத்தில் அரசியலமைப்பு ரீதியாக செய்யப்பட வேண்டும் என்று நினைக்கிறார்கள்.

ஜனாதிபதி ராஜினாமா செய்யாத வரையில் எந்த ஒரு மாற்றமும் நடக்காது என்று பல விமர்சகர்கள் நம்புகின்றனர். அரகலயா பிரதிநிதிகளும் கலந்து கொண்ட ஒரு செயலமர்வில், இந்தக் கருத்து வேறு எதையும் விட அதிகமாக வலியுறுத்தப்பட்டது. சிரமங்கள் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டபோது, ​​பிரதமர் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவின் பதவி நீக்கம் அரசாங்கத்தின் மீது கொண்டு வரப்பட்ட அழுத்தத்தின் வினைத்திறனுக்கான சான்றாகக் குறிப்பிட்ட அவர்கள், ஜனாதிபதியும் அவ்வாறே கையாளப்பட வேண்டும் என்று வலியுறுத்தினர்.

இலங்கை சட்டத்தரணிகள் சங்கம் (BASL) நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறைமையை முன்கூட்டியே நீக்க வேண்டுமென விரும்புகிறது. ஜனாதிபதி உடனடியாக பதவியை விட்டு வெளியேற வேண்டும் என பொதுமக்கள், சிவில் சமூகம் மற்றும் அரகலய ஆகியன கோரிக்கை விடுத்துள்ளன. ஜனாதிபதி பதவியை ஒழிக்க அல்லது/மற்றும் ராஜினாமா செய்ய வேண்டும் என்று பிரச்சாரம் செய்பவர்கள் ஏன் தோல்வியுற்ற ஜனாதிபதியை பதவியில் இருக்க அனுமதிக்க வேண்டும் என்று கேள்வி எழுப்புகின்றனர். ஆயினும்கூட, நடைமுறையில் உள்ள அரசியலமைப்பு சட்டத்தின் கீழ் இது கடினம். இதனால் ஜனாதிபதி வீட்டுக்கு செல்லுமாறு அரகலய கோரிக்கை விடுத்தது. ஜனாதிபதியை ஆதரிப்பவர்கள், அவர் 6.9 மில்லியன் மக்களால் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்டதாகக் கூறுகின்றனர், இருப்பினும் உண்மை அவர்களைத் தொந்தரவு செய்ய வேண்டும்.

20A நீக்கப்பட வேண்டும் என்று BASL கருதுவதாகவும், ஜனாதிபதியின் இராஜினாமா பற்றி குறிப்பிடவில்லை எனவும் பிரதமர் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க நம்புகிறார். உண்மை. BASL திட்டங்கள் மிகவும் செயலில் உள்ளன, ஆனால் அவை புனிதமானவை அல்ல. அரகலயாவின் கருத்துகளோ அல்லது சிவில் சமூகத்தின் பார்வைகளோ அதற்கானவை அல்ல! 21A நிறைவேற்றப்பட்ட பிறகு, 19A ஐ மீட்டெடுத்து, நாடாளுமன்றத்தை பலப்படுத்திய பிறகு, பிரதமர், அனைத்துக் கட்சித் தலைவர்கள் மற்றும் ஜனாதிபதி ஆகியோர் எதிர்கால நடவடிக்கையை தீர்மானிக்க வேண்டும் என்று பிரதமர் நினைக்கிறார்.

ஆனால் நீதி அமைச்சர் 19A முதல் 21A வரை மீட்டெடுக்க விரும்பவில்லை. எனவே, 21ஏ எவ்வாறு பாராளுமன்றத்தை பலப்படுத்துகிறது என்பது ஒரு பிரச்சினை. உதாரணமாக, முரட்டுத்தனமான தொழிலதிபர்கள் அல்லது "பத்திர முரடர்களை" காப்பாற்ற அல்லது மற்றொரு அரசியல்வாதியை வெறுக்க பாராளுமன்றம் தடைசெய்யப்படும் சூழ்நிலையை கற்பனை செய்து பாருங்கள். 21ஏ, குடியரசுத் தலைவருக்கு அவ்வாறு வழங்குவது, நாடாளுமன்றத்தைப் பலப்படுத்தும் என்பது வெறும் ஆசைதான். குற்றவாளிகளை மன்னிப்பதற்கான ஜனாதிபதியின் அதிகாரங்கள் உச்சநீதிமன்றத்தில் சவால் செய்யப்பட்ட நேரத்தில், BASL பரிந்துரைத்த காசோலைகள் மற்றும் சமநிலைகள் இல்லாமல் அத்தகைய அதிகாரத்தை தக்கவைத்துக்கொள்வதற்கான நகர்வுகள் அபத்தமானது.

சர்வதேச உதவியுடன் பொருளாதார மறுமலர்ச்சியை அடைவதற்கான முயற்சிகளின் வெற்றியானது பொருளாதார சீர்திருத்தங்களை மட்டுமல்ல, அரசியல் சீர்திருத்தங்களை செயல்படுத்துவதையும் சார்ந்துள்ளது. எனவே, "கட்டணச் சமநிலை நெருக்கடியைத் தீர்ப்பதற்கான வலுவான மற்றும் சாதகமான சூழல் நாட்டை கட்டமைப்புச் சீர்திருத்தங்களின் திட்டத்திற்கு வழிநடத்துவதாக இருக்கும்" என்ற அழைப்பில் தெளிவாகத் தெரிகிறது, இரண்டின் கலவையையும் ஆதரிக்கும் அணுகுமுறையின் தேவை. அவர்கள் கைகோர்த்து செல்ல வேண்டும், இல்லையெனில் அல்ல.

உதாரணமாக, IMF பணியாளர் அறிக்கை, நிதி நிலைத்தன்மையை மீட்டெடுப்பது, பாதிக்கப்படக்கூடியவர்களை பாதுகாத்தல் மற்றும் பணவியல் கொள்கை மற்றும் மாற்று விகித ஆட்சிகளின் நம்பகத்தன்மையை உறுதி செய்தல் பற்றி பேசுகிறது; நிதித்துறை ஸ்திரத்தன்மையை பாதுகாத்தல்; மேலும் வளர்ச்சியை அதிகரிக்கவும், நிர்வாகத்தை வலுப்படுத்தவும் கட்டமைப்பு சீர்திருத்தங்களைக் கூறுகிறது. எனவே பொருளாதார மற்றும் அரசியல் நிர்வாகம் IMF நிகழ்ச்சி நிரலில் இருப்பதைக் காணலாம்.

IMF தலைவர் Kristalina Georgieva நாம் இப்போது "தவறான நிர்வாகத்தின் விளைவு" என்று குறிப்பிட்டுள்ளார், மேலும் தீவு தேசத்தை மீண்டும் ஒரு நல்ல பெரிய பொருளாதார அடித்தளத்தில் வைப்பதே மிக முக்கியமான விஷயம். மேக்ரோ எகனாமிக் அடித்தளத்தை யார் முறியடித்தார்கள் மற்றும் யார் 'தவறுகளை' ஒப்புக்கொண்டார்கள், எனவே தவறான நிர்வாகத்திற்கு யார் பொறுப்பு என்பதை நாங்கள் அறிவோம். தற்போதைய குழப்பத்திற்கு காரணமானவர்கள் பொருளாதாரத்தை தவறாக நிர்வகிப்பதற்கு அதே அதிகாரங்களைப் பயன்படுத்துவதற்கு 21A முயற்சிக்கிறது.

பொருளாதார ஸ்திரத்தன்மையை மீட்டெடுப்பதற்கான பொருத்தமான கொள்கைகள் குறித்து இலங்கைக்கு ஆலோசனை வழங்குவதில் சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியம் மற்றும் ஏனைய அபிவிருத்தி பங்காளிகளுடன் இணைந்து செயற்படுவதாகவும் இலங்கைக்கு புதிய நிதியுதவி வழங்குவதற்கு திட்டமிடவில்லை எனவும் உலக வங்கி தெரிவித்துள்ளது. மேக்ரோ பொருளாதாரக் கொள்கை கட்டமைப்பில் அரசியல் நிர்வாக சீர்திருத்தங்களும் அடங்கும்.

மக்கள் விரும்பும் அரசியல் சீர்திருத்தங்களை மேற்கொண்டு நாட்டில் ஜனநாயகத்தை பாதுகாக்க வேண்டியதன் அவசியத்தை அமெரிக்க தூதுவர் ஜூலி சுங் சபாநாயகருடனான சந்திப்பில் வலியுறுத்தியுள்ளார். புதிய பிரதமர் உட்பட அரசாங்கம் அரசியல் ஸ்திரத்தன்மையை ஏற்படுத்தவும் தற்போதைய பொருளாதார நெருக்கடியை சமாளிக்கவும் முடியும் என தான் நம்புவதாக தூதுவர் கூறியுள்ளார்.

USAID இன் சமந்தா பவர் நிர்வாகி இலங்கையர்களுக்கு தனது ஆதரவை உறுதியளித்தார், மேலும் USAID நாடு நெருக்கடியை சமாளிக்க உதவும் என்று உறுதியளித்ததுடன், அரசியல் மற்றும் பொருளாதார சீர்திருத்தங்களை அவசரமாக மேற்கொள்ள வேண்டியதன் அவசியத்தையும் வலியுறுத்தினார். IMF, உலக வங்கி, G7 போன்ற பிற நன்கொடையாளர்களுடன் இலங்கைக்கு ஆதரவாக நெருக்கமாக பணியாற்றும் சமந்தா பவரின் சக்தி உறுதியானது, ஆனால் அவரது மேற்கூறிய கவலைகள் அவரது சிந்தனையை பாதிக்கும்.

இலங்கையின் ஜனநாயகம், ஸ்திரத்தன்மை மற்றும் பொருளாதார மீட்சிக்கு இந்தியா தொடர்ந்தும் இலங்கையர்களுடன் துணை நிற்கும் என இந்திய பிரதமர் நரேந்திர மோடி தெரிவித்துள்ளார். பொருளாதார மறுமலர்ச்சியுடன் அரசியல் ஸ்திரத்தன்மையையும் இணைத்தார்.

அனைத்து வெளிநாட்டு பிரமுகர்களும் அரசியல் ஸ்திரத்தன்மையின் அவசியத்தை வலியுறுத்தினர், ஆனால் ஸ்ரீலங்கா பொதுஜன பெரமுனவின் பொதுச் செயலாளர் காரியவசம் வேறுவிதமாக நம்புகிறார். இது அவரது அரசியல் முதலாளியைப் பாதுகாப்பதற்காகவே என்பது புரிந்துகொள்ளத்தக்கது. அரசியல் சீர்திருத்தங்களுக்கு முன்னுரிமை அளித்த ஜனாதிபதியிடம் கூட அவர் முரண்படுகிறார். இந்தப் பின்னணியில், நிலைமையை தாம் கட்டுப்பாட்டில் வைத்திருப்பதை நிரூபிக்க வேண்டிய பொறுப்பு பிரதமர் விக்ரமசிங்கவிடம் உள்ளது.

21A தொடர்பாக அரசாங்கம் எதிர்கொள்ளும் பிரச்சனைகள் மிகவும் சிக்கலானவை. அரசாங்கக் குழுவிற்குள்ளும் முரண்பட்ட கோரிக்கைகள் உள்ளன. சிவில் சமூகம் ஒரே குரலில் பேசுவதில்லை. ஜனாதிபதியின் விருப்பம் 21A இல் பிரதிபலிக்கிறது, ஏனெனில் இது அவரது ஒப்புதலுடன் முன்மொழியப்பட்டது என்று அவர் ஒப்புக்கொண்டார். 20A இல் எந்தவொரு ஜனாதிபதியும் தானாக முன்வந்து அதிகாரங்களை விட்டுக்கொடுக்க மாட்டார்கள். அரசியல் அதிகாரத்தைப் பற்றி விவாதிக்கும்போது, ​​‘அரசியல்வாதிகளின் சக்தி’யில் ஒரு மேற்கோள் நினைவுக்கு வருகிறது.

“அதிகாரத்தைத் தக்கவைத்துக்கொள்ள வேண்டும் என்ற எண்ணம் ஒருபோதும் மறைந்துவிடாது, அதன் காரணமாகவே நமது அரசியல் தலைவர்கள் ஊழலுக்கு ஆளாகிறார்கள். … ஜனநாயக முறையில் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்ட ஒரு அரசியல்வாதிக்கு, ஒவ்வொரு கொள்கை வளர்ச்சிக்கும், ஞானத்திற்கான ஒவ்வொரு விருப்பத்துக்கும் பக்கபலமாக நடப்பது, அதிகாரத்தைப் பெறுவதில் அல்லது தக்கவைத்துக் கொள்வதில் அதன் விளைவு என்னவாக இருக்கும் என்ற சிந்தனைதான்.

இது கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச, ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க, சஜித் பிரேமதாச மற்றும் பலருக்கும் விதிவிலக்கு இல்லாமல் பொருந்தும். அதிகாரத்தைப் பெறுவதற்கும், ஆட்சியைத் தக்கவைப்பதற்கும் அவர்கள் எல்லாவற்றையும் செய்வார்கள். எனவே, பல சூழ்ச்சிகளுடன், அமைச்சர் விஜயதாச ராஜபக்ஷ 21A க்கு மூன்றில் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மையைப் பெறலாம், இரட்டைக் குடியுரிமை பெற்றவர்கள் அரசியல் பதவிகளில் அமர்வதைத் தடுப்பது போன்ற விதிகளை சமரசம் செய்து, ஜனாதிபதி மற்றும் பிரதமர் பசிலின் நகர்வுகளை தோற்கடிக்காத பட்சத்தில். .

21A ஐ அமைச்சர் இராஜபக்ஷவும் பிரதமரும் ஒப்புதலுக்கு முன் மீளாய்வு செய்வது வெகுஜன எதிர்ப்பைத் தடுக்கலாம். சிவில் சமூகம் மற்றும் அரகலயாவில் உள்ளவர்களும் "அரசியலமைப்பு மூலம் நரகத்திற்கு" என்று கூறாமல் இந்த நடைமுறை நடைமுறைகளை கருத்தில் கொள்ள வேண்டும். ஜனநாயக ரீதியில் மாற்றங்களைக் கொண்டுவரும் ஆற்றலை அரகலயா பெறவும் தக்கவைத்துக் கொள்ளவும் வேண்டும், எனவே மேற்கூறிய மேற்கோள் அதற்கும் பொருந்தும்.

எலைன் கிளாசர் எதிர்ப்பு அரசியலில் சொல்வதிலிருந்து அனைத்து அரசியல்வாதிகளும் பாடம் கற்க முடியும்: "... அரசியல் என்பது அதிகார வரம்பின் பொதுவான தன்மையைப் பற்றியது, விஷயங்கள் எவ்வாறு செயல்படுகின்றன என்பதை கூட்டாக ஒழுங்கமைக்க ஒரு சமூக விருப்பம்- ஒற்றை, ஒப்புக்கொண்ட விஷயங்களைச் செய்ய வேண்டும்." தற்போது நாம் அத்தகைய அரசியலுக்கு இலக்காகவில்லை மாறாக சூழ்ச்சித்தனமான வக்கிரமாக இருக்கிறோம். இந்த அறிக்கையை அனைத்து அரசியல் குழுக்களும் கேட்கட்டும்.

நான் சட்டத்தரணி இல்லையென்றாலும், பசில் ராஜபக்ஷவிற்கும் நிறுவனத்திற்கும் ஒரு தடையாக எனது உள்ளுணர்வைக் காண்கிறேன். கோத்தபய ராஜபக்ஷ அரசாங்கத்தால் 20A அறிமுகப்படுத்தப்பட்டபோது அது கொண்டு வரப்பட்ட 78(3) சரத்தில் உள்ளது. அது பின்வருமாறு கூறுகிறது: "(3) பாராளுமன்றத்தில் ஒரு மசோதாவிற்கு முன்மொழியப்படும் எந்தவொரு திருத்தமும் அந்த மசோதாவின் தகுதிகள் மற்றும் கொள்கைகளிலிருந்து விலகாது."

விஜேயதாச ராஜபக்ஷவோ அல்லது பசில் ராஜபக்ச ஆதரவாளரோ அரசியலமைப்பு ரீதியாக மூன்றில் இரண்டு பங்கு அனுமதியைப் பெறுவதற்கான "முன்மொழிவை" (முன்னாள் தினுக்குரலில் கூறியது போல்) திரும்பப் பெற முயற்சித்தால், அது எதிர்க்கட்சிகளுக்கு வீணான, புனிதமான உரிமையாக இருக்கும். . மசோதாவின் "தகுதிகள்" மற்றும் "கொள்கைகள்" முடிவு செய்யப்படும் போது அது ஹவுஸ் அல்லது நீதிமன்றத்தால் ஒரு நாள் முடிவு செய்யப்படும். சபையில், தகுதி மற்றும் கொள்கைகள் ஆட்சி செய்ய வேண்டும் என்று நான் எதிர்பார்க்கவில்லை. எவ்வாறாயினும், இரட்டைக் குடியுரிமை பெற்றவர்கள் அரசியல் பதவிகளை வகிக்கும் வகையில், குழுநிலையில் எவரும் திருத்தம் கொண்டு வருவதைத் தடுக்க, நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர்கள் இந்த ஏற்பாட்டைப் பயன்படுத்திக்கொள்ளலாம். நம் விரல்களை குறுக்காக வைத்திருப்போம்.

ஹிருணிகா பிரேமச்சந்திர அவர்கள் மிரிஹானவில் ஆரம்பித்து வைத்த காரியத்தின் கேரி ஓவர் என்ற வகையில் அவர்களுக்கு எதிரான விமர்சனங்களையும் சிவில் சமூகம் கவனத்தில் கொள்ள வேண்டும். 21A க்கு மூன்றில் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மையைப் பெறுவதற்காக, ஜனாதிபதியின் அதிகாரங்களை பின்னர் குறைப்பதாக வாக்குறுதியளித்து, அரசியல்வாதிகள் அதை சவாரி செய்ய அனுமதிக்கக்கூடாது என்பதை அது அறிந்திருக்க வேண்டும். அரசாங்கத்திலோ அல்லது எதிர்க்கட்சியிலோ இந்த கொடூரமான அதிகாரங்களைப் பயன்படுத்த விரும்பினாலும் அரசியலில் பலர் இது நடக்காது, மேலும் முக்கியமான கட்டத்தில் பின்வாங்குவார்கள், ஏனென்றால் மேலே மேற்கோள் காட்டப்பட்டுள்ளபடி, அவர்கள் அதிகாரத்தை 'ஆதாயம்' மற்றும் 'தக்கவைக்க' வேண்டும்.

பிரதமர் விக்கிரமசிங்கவிடம் ஒருவர் கூறலாம், “ஐயா, நல்லாட்சியை யதார்த்தமாக்குவதற்கு இதுவே உங்களுக்குக் கிடைத்த கடைசி சந்தர்ப்பம், சூழ்ச்சிகளால் அதனைத் துடைக்க அனுமதிக்காதீர்கள். நீங்கள் இப்போது அதை அடையவில்லை என்றால், நீங்கள் ஜனநாயக நல்லாட்சி விழுமியங்களுக்கு எதிராக பாவம் செய்த தோல்வி என்று அழைக்கப்படுவீர்கள், அதற்காக நீங்கள் சர்வதேச அரங்கில் கூட கவனிக்கப்படுகிறீர்கள்.

en.title: The success of efforts being made to achieve economic revival with international assistance hinges on not only economic reforms but also the implementation of political reforms.
body_lines: Proponents of constitutional economic reforms are struggling to prioritise solutions for the current socio-economic-politico imbroglio. Pohottuwa General Secretary Sagara Kariyawasam has said the economic crisis should be resolved first and then an environment created for constitutional amendments. President Gotabaya Rajapaksa banks on the Romesh de Silva Committee for the drafting of a new Constitution. The Janatha Vimukthi Peramuna, Tamil National Alliance, and Samagi Jana Balavegaya have prioritised enhanced wider constitutional reforms.

The Government is allergic to very radical changes demanded by the Gota Go Home protesters et al. Due to intense pressure, the 21st Amendment (21A) has been tabled to revive the 19th Amendment (19A). To my mind, it is a half-baked 19A Minus. It has diluted 19A, which, among other things, prevented the President from holding portfolios and limited the number of Cabinet ministers. The President has brought 42 institutions under the Ministry of Defence through the latest gazette, while admitting that he has made serious mistakes, probably disqualifying him from taking over so many responsibilities.

What the pro-democracy activists are demanding are far-reaching changes such as the President being stripped of immunity and powers to dissolve and prorogue parliament, the pardoning of convicted offenders, etc. It is well-nigh impossible for the 225 MPs to move an impeachment motion to rid of any failed President, but the latter can dissolve the parliament at the stroke of a pen!

Further, new demands are in circulation, e.g., the creation of a National Policy-Making Council, strengthening public service through depoliticisation, enhancing financial accountability (Article 148) through Committee on Public Enterprises, Committee on Public Accounts, Committee on Public Finance, etc. in the Parliament, the appointment of the Monetary Board and the Governor of the Central Bank with Constitutional Council approval, the appointment of the Ministry Secretaries, Provincial Governors, Ambassadors, et al on the advice of the PM in consultation with the Cabinet, etc. 21A does not incorporate any of these and still, government politicians and some civil society spokespersons consider 21A is the best.

One controversial demand is barring dual citizens from holding public office. A section of the Pohottuwa Group opposes this since MP Basil Rajapaksa will be affected. For instance, MP Sagara Kariyawasam, the General Secretary of Pohottuwa has said that the Constitution should not be designed to target specific individuals. He has overlooked the fact that 20A removed the bar on dual citizens for the benefit of a single individual, namely the same Basil Rajapaksa.

In a lighter vein, it is noted that many of those who are opposed to dual citizens holding public office have Prime Ministerial/Presidential dreams and consider Basil Rajapaksa as a stumbling block to them. They fish in troubled waters!

Controversial proposals of this nature had to be withdrawn to ensure a two-thirds majority when the 19A was approved by Parliament. I think something similar might happen this time around as well. Thinukural reports that Minister Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe has stated that the “provision on preventing dual citizenship holders from entering parliament is merely a proposal” may be a bargaining signal to withdraw it at the Committee Stage for a two-thirds majority to be mustered. History may repeat itself, and Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe will be the saviour.

If 21A does not receive a two-thirds majority, the President may be the happiest. Some civil society persons, without being critical of attempts to dilute the 19A, claim they accessed the country’s highest political leaders, and everyone agreed with their proposals. But concurrently we hear dissenting voices from the latter, making us wonder who is telling the truth.

Many of the proponents of constitutional reforms steadfastly believe that the economic collapse was due to political mismanagement, exacerbated by 20A, which led to the concentration of too much power in the executive presidency. They insist on reinstating the 19A even with its weaknesses. Another reason is the conviction that 21A is a halfway measure aimed at strengthening the position of the President. In the meantime, some demand a total system change and think it should be done constitutionally in one go.

Several critics believe that no system change could happen unless the President resigns. At a workshop attended also by Aragalaya representatives, this view was emphasised more than anything else. When difficulties were mentioned, they cited the removal of PM Mahinda Rajapaksa as proof of the effectiveness of pressure brought to bear on the Government and insisted the President, too, should be similarly dealt with.

The Bar Association of Sri Lanka (BASL) wants the Executive Presidency abolished early. The public, civil society, and the Aragalaya demand that the President leave office immediately. Those canvassing for the abolition of the presidency or/and resignation question why a failed President should be allowed to be in office. Nevertheless, it is difficult under the prevailing constitutional law. This made the Aragalaya demand that the President go home. Those who are supportive of the President claim that he was elected by 6.9 million people, though the reality must be troubling them.

PM Ranil Wickremesinghe believes that the BASL is of the view that the 20A must be abolished and does not mention the President’s resignation. True. BASL proposals are extremely proactive, but they are not sacrosanct. Nor are the views of the Aragalaya or civil society views for that matter! The PM thinks that after the passage of the 21A, having restored the 19A, and strengthening the Parliament, the PM, all party leaders, and the President must decide a future course of action.

But the Minister of Justice does not want to restore 19A through 21A. Hence, how 21A strengthens the Parliament is an issue. For example, imagine a situation where the Parliament is prorogued to save roguish businesspeople or “bond rogues” or to spite another politician. It is only wishful thinking that 21A, which provides for the President to do so, will strengthen the Parliament. At a time when the President’s powers to pardon convicts have been challenged before the Supreme Court, moves to retain such power without checks and balances suggested by the BASL are absurd.

The success of efforts being made to achieve economic revival with international assistance hinges on not only economic reforms but also the implementation of political reforms. Hence the need for an approach, which supports the combination of both, as evident in the call for “a strong and conducive environment for resolving the balance of payments crisis would be to direct the country to a program of structural reforms.” They must go hand in hand, and not otherwise.

For instance, the IMF Staff Statement speaks of restoring fiscal sustainability, protecting the vulnerable and ensuring credibility of the monetary policy and exchange rate regimes; preserving financial sector stability; and states structural reforms to enhance growth and strengthen governance. Hence it can be seen that economic and political governance is on the IMF agenda.

IMF Chief Kristalina Georgieva has stated that what we undergo now is “a result of mismanagement,” and the most important thing to do is to put the island nation back on a sound macroeconomic footing. We know who bungled the macroeconomic footing and who admitted ‘mistakes’ and hence was responsible for mismanagement. The 21A tries to enable those responsible for the current mess to exercise the same powers to mismanage the economy.

The World Bank has said that it works with the IMF and other development partners in advising Sri Lanka on appropriate policies to restore economic stability until an adequate macroeconomic policy framework is in place and does not plan to offer new financing to Sri Lanka. The macroeconomic policy framework will invariably include political governance reforms too.

US Ambassador Julie Chung, at a meeting with the Speaker, has emphasised the need to carry out political reforms desired by the people and to safeguard democracy in the country. The Ambassador has said she hopes the government, including the new PM, will be able to bring about political stability and overcome the current economic crisis.

Samantha Power Administrator of USAID pledged her support to Sri Lankans and committed that USAID would help the country weather the crisis and concurrently stressed the need to urgently undertake political and economic reforms. Samantha Power’s power when she works closely with other donors such as the IMF, the World Bank, G7, and others to support Sri Lanka is assured, but her aforesaid concerns will influence her thinking.

Indian PM Narendra Modi has stated that India will continue to stand with Sri Lankans and support democracy, stability, and economic recovery in Sri Lanka. He also combined political stability with economic revival.

All foreign dignitaries have stressed the need for political stability, but SLPP General Secretary Kariyawasam is convinced otherwise. Understandably, this is to defend his political boss. He contradicts even the President who has prioritised political reforms. Against this backdrop, the onus is on PM Wickremesinghe to prove that he is in control of the situation.

The problems faced by the Government in respect of 21A are very complex. There are conflicting demands even within the Government group. Civil society does not speak with one voice. The President’s wishes are reflected in 21A, for he has confessed that it was proposed with his consent. No President will voluntarily give up powers in 20A. Discussing political power I am reminded of a quote in ‘The Power of Politicians.’

“The need to seek and retain power never goes away, and our political leaders are vulnerable to corruption just by virtue of that. … For a democratically elected politician, walking alongside every policy development, every wish for wisdom, is the thought of what its effect will be on gaining or retaining in power.”

This applies to Gotabaya Rajapaksa, Ranil Wickremesinghe, Sajith Premadasa, et al without exception. They will do everything to gain and retain power. Hence, with many manipulations, Minister Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe may be able to secure a two-thirds majority for 21A, after compromising the provisions such as those that prevent dual citizens from holding political office, unless the President and the PM defeat moves being made by Basil.

Reviewing 21A by Minister Rajapakshe and the PM before ratification could prevent mass resistance. Those in civil society and Aragalaya also should consider these practicalities of implementation without saying “To hell with the Constitution.” Aragalaya also needs to gain and retain the power to bring about changes democratically and hence the above-mentioned quote applies to it as well.

All politicians can learn a lesson from what Elaine Glaser says in Anti-Politics: “… politics is about a generality of jurisdiction, a social desire to collectively organise how things work- to have a single, agreed way of doing things.” At present we are not destined for such politics but manipulative crookedness. Let this statement be heard by all political groups.

Though I am no lawyer, to my intuition I find one roadblock to Basil Rajapaksa and company. It is in Article 78(3) which was introduced by the Gotabaya Rajapaksa government when 20A was introduced. It says as follows: “(3) Any amendment proposed to a Bill in Parliament shall not deviate from the merits and principles of that Bill.”

It will be the inviolable, sacrosanct right for the Opposition to harp on it if Wijeyadasa Rajapakshe or a Basil Rajapaksa supporter attempts to withdraw the “proposal” (as the former has stated in Thinukural) to be constitutionally crooked to obtain two-thirds at approval. It will be decided by the House or a Court of Law one day when the “merits” and “principles” of the Bill are decided. In the House, I do not expect merit and principles to reign. Anyway, Parliamentarians may make use of this provision to prevent anyone from bringing an amendment at the Committee Stage enabling dual citizens to hold political office. Let us keep our fingers crossed.

Civil society also should mind the criticism against them as carry-overs of what Hirunika Premachandra started at Mirihana. It must be aware that it should not allow the politicians to take it for a ride with a promise to reduce presidential powers later, to secure a two-thirds majority for the 21A. It will not happen as many in politics whether in the Government or the Opposition aspire to use these draconian powers and will fall back at the crucial juncture because as quoted above, they need to ‘gain’ and ‘retain’ power.

To PM Wickremesinghe one may say, “Sir, this is the last opportunity for you to make good governance a reality, and do not allow it to be whisked away by manipulations. If you do not achieve it now, you will be called a failure who sinned against democratic good governance values, for which you are beholden even in the international arena.”
